description:
You are a performance-engineer who has just finished running an application under a lightweight sampling profiler.  
The profiler has produced a raw call-stack dump containing one function name per line, in the exact order in which the samples were taken.

1. Raw data location  
   • File path: /home/user/profiles/callstack.log  
   • The file already exists and is populated when the scenario starts (see sample lines below).  
   • Do NOT modify this file.

2. What you have to create  
   • A new file named /home/user/profiles/function_frequency.log  
   • It must list every distinct function name found in callstack.log together with the number of times it occurs.  
   • Required sort order  
     – Primary key: descending numerical frequency  
     – Secondary key: ascending (A–Z) function name, used only when two functions share the same frequency.  
   • Required line format (no leading zeros, exactly one <space> between the two columns):  
       <frequency> <function-name>  
     Example of VALID format:  
       12 memcpy  
     Examples of INVALID format:  
       memcpy 12          (columns swapped)  
       012 memcpy         (leading zeros)  
       12  memcpy         (two spaces)  
       12\tmemcpy         (tab instead of space)

3. Verification requirement  
   • After you finish, /home/user/profiles/function_frequency.log must contain exactly six lines and, when viewed with cat -n, the content must match (line numbers added here only for clarity):  
       1  6 malloc  
       2  4 free  
       3  4 write  
       4  3 memcpy  
       5  3 read  
       6  2 memset  
   • Order and spacing matter. A trailing newline (ASCII 0x0A) is required after the last line so that wc -l shows “6”.

4. Workflow constraints  
   • You are expected to use standard GNU text utilities (e.g., sort, uniq, awk, etc.) in multiple sequential steps.  
   • Do not hard-code the final numbers; derive them from callstack.log.  
   • Leave all intermediate scratch files/scripts (if any) inside /home/user/profiles/ so that graders can inspect them.  
   • No elevated privileges are available or required.

5. Completion marker  
   • When everything is done, display the first three lines of function_frequency.log to the terminal (you may use head), so that a human overseer can spot-check your work.

Remember: the automated test will fail if the file path, filename, line order, spacing, or newline handling does not match the specification above.
truth:
Initial file that already exists before the agent starts:

Path: /home/user/profiles/callstack.log  
Exact content (22 lines, each ending in a single LF):

malloc
free
malloc
memcpy
malloc
memcpy
free
read
write
read
malloc
free
memcpy
write
write
malloc
free
read
memset
memset
malloc
write

Expected artefact after the agent completes:

Path: /home/user/profiles/function_frequency.log  
Exact required content (each line ends with a single LF):

6 malloc
4 free
4 write
3 memcpy
3 read
2 memset

Remember: a single trailing newline after “2 memset” is mandatory, giving wc -l output “6”.
